lessons.title: Identify a Cisco Switch
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated software, uptime, and platform details.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before troubleshooting so you know the simulated platform.
lessons.good_output: Expected platform and stable uptime are shown.
lessons.bad_output: Unexpected platform or repeated reload evidence needs review.
lessons.common_mistakes: Treating the version command as a fix.
lessons.practice_output:
<SWITCH_NAME># show version
Cisco IOS simulated switch
Uptime: <UPTIME>
lessons.final_summary: Identify the platform before making a decision.

lessons.title: Read Cisco Interface Status
lessons.meaning: Shows summary state, VLAN, speed, and duplex for simulated ports.
lessons.when_to_use: Use as the first read-only check for a port issue.
lessons.good_output: <ACCESS_PORT> is connected on the intended VLAN.
lessons.bad_output: notconnect, disabled, err-disabled, or an unexpected VLAN needs investigation.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing a port before confirming its state.
lessons.practice_output:
Port  Name  Status  Vlan
<ACCESS_PORT>  <DEVICE_NAME>  connected  <VLAN_ID>
lessons.final_summary: Start with observed port state.

lessons.title: Read VLAN Membership
lessons.meaning: Lists VLANs and simulated access-port membership.
lessons.when_to_use: Use to confirm an access port belongs to the intended VLAN.
lessons.good_output: <VLAN_ID> is active and includes <ACCESS_PORT>.
lessons.bad_output: The port is absent or listed in an unexpected VLAN.
lessons.common_mistakes: Assuming a VLAN exists because a port command accepted it.
lessons.next_commands: show vlan id <VLAN_ID>; show running-config interface <ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.practice_output:
VLAN  Name  Status  Ports
<VLAN_ID>  <VLAN_NAME>  active  <ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.final_summary: VLAN membership is evidence, not an assumption.

lessons.title: Trace a Learned MAC Address
lessons.meaning: Shows whether a simulated endpoint MAC address is learned on a port.
lessons.when_to_use: Use to confirm that a device is communicating at Layer 2.
lessons.good_output: <MAC_ADDRESS> is dynamic on <ACCESS_PORT>.
lessons.bad_output: No learned MAC or the MAC on an unexpected port requires investigation.
lessons.common_mistakes: Assuming a learned MAC proves the endpoint application works.
lessons.practice_task: Find the simulated MAC learning record.
lessons.practice_output:
Vlan  Mac Address  Type  Ports
<VLAN_ID>  <MAC_ADDRESS>  DYNAMIC  <ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.final_summary: MAC learning helps locate an endpoint.

lessons.title: Read IP and ARP Evidence
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated IP-to-MAC mappings.
lessons.when_to_use: Use when tracing a host, gateway, or VLAN path.
lessons.good_output: <GATEWAY_IP> maps to a stable MAC address.
lessons.bad_output: Missing or unexpected ARP entries need controlled follow-up.
lessons.common_mistakes: Clearing ARP while still collecting evidence.
lessons.practice_output:
Protocol  Address  Hardware Addr  Interface
Internet  <GATEWAY_IP>  <MAC_ADDRESS>  <VLAN_ID>
lessons.final_summary: ARP connects an IP address to Layer 2 evidence.

lessons.title: Discover a Neighbor
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated directly connected neighbor information.
lessons.when_to_use: Use to understand which uplink or endpoint is attached.
lessons.good_output: Expected neighbor and local port are present.
lessons.bad_output: Missing or unexpected neighbor information needs verification.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing an uplink before confirming its neighbor.
lessons.next_commands: show cdp neighbors detail; show lldp neighbors
lessons.practice_output:
Device ID  Local Interface  Capability
<DEVICE_NAME>  <TRUNK_PORT>  Switch
lessons.final_summary: Neighbor discovery is a read-only topology clue.

lessons.title: Inspect Trunk and STP State
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated trunk, spanning-tree, or port-channel state.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before changing an uplink or aggregation.
lessons.good_output: <TRUNK_PORT> carries the intended VLANs and STP is forwarding as designed.
lessons.bad_output: A missing allowed VLAN, blocking state, or suspended bundle needs analysis.
lessons.common_mistakes: Forcing a trunk or STP role without topology evidence.
lessons.practice_output:
Port  Mode  Status  Vlans allowed
<TRUNK_PORT>  on  trunking  <VLAN_ID>
lessons.final_summary: Trunks, STP, and aggregation require careful evidence.

lessons.title: Read Comware IRF Members
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated IRF members, roles, priorities, and status.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before considering any IRF or stack action.
lessons.good_output: Expected <STACK_MEMBER_ID> members are present with healthy roles.
lessons.bad_output: Missing members or an unexpected role requires escalation and evidence.
lessons.common_mistakes: Resetting IRF because a member looks unusual.
lessons.practice_task: Read the simulated IRF member view.
lessons.practice_output:
MemberID  Role  Priority  Status
<STACK_MEMBER_ID>  Master  <PRIORITY>  Ready
lessons.final_summary: IRF state must be observed before any change.

lessons.title: Read Comware IRF Topology
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated stack-link topology and health.
lessons.when_to_use: Use after display irf to collect link evidence.
lessons.good_output: Expected IRF links are UP.
lessons.bad_output: An IRF link DOWN requires safe physical and change-process investigation.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing membership while a stack incident is active.
lessons.practice_output: Member <STACK_MEMBER_ID>  <IRF_PORT>  UP
lessons.final_summary: Link evidence comes before stack remediation.

lessons.title: Read Windows Network Details
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated adapter, address, gateway, DNS, and DHCP data.
lessons.when_to_use: Use first when a Windows endpoint cannot reach a network service.
lessons.good_output: An expected address, <GATEWAY_IP>, and <DNS_IP> are present.
lessons.bad_output: APIPA or a missing gateway indicates an addressing-path problem.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing static IP settings before collecting evidence.
lessons.practice_output:
IPv4 Address: <HOST_IP>
Default Gateway: <GATEWAY_IP>
DNS Servers: <DNS_IP>
lessons.final_summary: Read address, gateway, and DNS together.

lessons.title: Read Linux Address and Route
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated Linux address or default-route state.
lessons.when_to_use: Use to understand local interface and gateway configuration.
lessons.good_output: The intended interface has an address and a default route via <GATEWAY_IP>.
lessons.bad_output: No address, no carrier, or no default route requires follow-up.
lessons.common_mistakes: Restarting a service before reading its state.
lessons.practice_output:
<INTERFACE>  state UP
inet <HOST_IP>/<PREFIX_LENGTH>
lessons.final_summary: Address and route evidence should agree.

lessons.title: Recognize a Dangerous Command
lessons.meaning: These commands can interrupt service or remove configuration.
lessons.when_to_use: Recognize risk before entering any destructive or service-impacting command.
lessons.good_output: The command is stopped and a safety review is requested.
lessons.bad_output: Executing without approval, backup, and rollback planning.
lessons.common_mistakes: Treating lab permissions as production approval.
lessons.practice_task: Enter reload to see the simulated safety stop.
lessons.practice_output: Safety stop: destructive commands are blocked in Lab Mode.
lessons.final_summary: Stop, explain the risk, and seek approval.

lessons.title: Configure an Access Port
lessons.meaning: Enters simulated configuration mode for a controlled access-port change.
lessons.when_to_use: Only after port, VLAN, approval, and rollback are confirmed.
lessons.good_output: <ACCESS_PORT> is access mode on <VLAN_ID> and no shutdown.
lessons.bad_output: Wrong port, wrong VLAN, or an unverified change.
lessons.common_mistakes: Saving before the verification command.
lessons.practice_output: Enter configuration mode. Changes affect only this local simulation.
lessons.final_summary: Configure, verify, then save or roll back.

lessons.title: Configure a Trunk Port
lessons.meaning: Starts a simulated trunk configuration workflow.
lessons.when_to_use: Only with confirmed topology, allowed VLANs, and change approval.
lessons.good_output: <TRUNK_PORT> is trunking with the intended allowed VLANs.
lessons.bad_output: An access port is accidentally converted or a required VLAN is missing.
lessons.common_mistakes: Applying trunk configuration to an endpoint port.
lessons.practice_task: Enter configuration mode for the simulated trunk lesson.
lessons.practice_output: Enter configuration mode. Changes affect only this local simulation.
lessons.final_summary: Uplinks demand topology validation.

lessons.title: Add an Interface Description
lessons.meaning: Selects an interface for a simulated descriptive label.
lessons.when_to_use: After confirming the intended port.
lessons.good_output: The description identifies <DEVICE_NAME> without changing service state.
lessons.bad_output: A misleading description on the wrong interface.
lessons.common_mistakes: Using a description as proof of physical connectivity.
lessons.practice_output: Selected simulated interface <ACCESS_PORT>.
lessons.final_summary: Labels improve future troubleshooting.

lessons.title: Shutdown and No Shutdown Safety
lessons.meaning: Administratively disables or enables a simulated port.
lessons.when_to_use: Only with explicit scope, approval, and a recovery plan.
lessons.good_output: The intended simulated port state is verified after the planned action.
lessons.bad_output: An outage caused by touching the wrong port.
lessons.common_mistakes: Using shut/no shut as a first diagnostic step.
lessons.next_commands: show interface status; show running-config interface <ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.practice_output: Simulated interface state changed. Verify before saving.
lessons.hint: Use this only in the controlled simulated lesson.
lessons.final_summary: Administrative state changes require a safety check.

lessons.title: Verify Before Saving
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated configuration for verification.
lessons.when_to_use: After a simulated change and before a save.
lessons.good_output: Mode, VLAN, description, and admin state match the intended change.
lessons.bad_output: Any mismatch means roll back or correct before saving.
lessons.common_mistakes: Assuming a successful configuration command proves the final state.
lessons.practice_output:
interface <ACCESS_PORT>
 switchport access vlan <VLAN_ID>
 no shutdown
lessons.final_summary: Verification is the decision point before save.

lessons.title: Roll Back a Simulated Change
lessons.meaning: Returns the simulated device to its initial local state.
lessons.when_to_use: Use when a simulated change must be abandoned or repeated.
lessons.good_output: The baseline simulated configuration is restored.
lessons.bad_output: Trying destructive production commands instead of a controlled rollback.
lessons.common_mistakes: Using reload as a rollback plan.
lessons.practice_output: Simulated device rolled back to its baseline.
lessons.final_summary: A rollback plan comes before a risky change.

lessons.title: Identify an HP Comware Switch
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated Comware platform and software details.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before reading interfaces or stack state.
lessons.good_output: Expected platform and stable system information appear.
lessons.bad_output: Unexpected software or repeated restart evidence needs review.
lessons.common_mistakes: Treating a version check as a repair.
lessons.practice_output:
<SWITCH_NAME>
Comware simulated platform
Version: <SOFTWARE_VERSION>
lessons.final_summary: Identify the switch family before troubleshooting.

lessons.title: Read a Comware Interface
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated Comware interface link state.
lessons.when_to_use: Use as a safe first check for an HP Comware port issue.
lessons.good_output: <HP_ACCESS_PORT> is up with the expected status.
lessons.bad_output: DOWN or administratively disabled requires controlled follow-up.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing port state before reading its configuration.
lessons.practice_task: Read the simulated Comware port summary.
lessons.practice_output:
Interface  Link  Speed
<HP_ACCESS_PORT>  UP  <SPEED>
lessons.final_summary: Use the vendor-native read-only command first.

lessons.title: Configure a Comware Access Port
lessons.meaning: Enters simulated Comware configuration mode.
lessons.when_to_use: Only after confirming <HP_ACCESS_PORT>, <VLAN_ID>, approval, and rollback.
lessons.good_output: <HP_ACCESS_PORT> is access mode in <VLAN_ID> and verified.
lessons.bad_output: Wrong interface or VLAN, or an unverified save.
lessons.common_mistakes: Using save force before verification.
lessons.next_commands: interface <HP_ACCESS_PORT>; port access vlan <VLAN_ID>; display vlan <VLAN_ID>
lessons.practice_task: Begin a controlled Comware simulated configuration.
lessons.practice_output: Enter configuration mode. Changes affect only this local simulation.
lessons.final_summary: Comware changes also follow configure, verify, then save or roll back.

lessons.title: Identify an ArubaOS-CX Switch
lessons.meaning: Shows simulated ArubaOS-CX system and software information.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before diagnosing interface, VLAN, or LACP state.
lessons.good_output: Expected software and platform information are present.
lessons.bad_output: Unexpected platform information needs confirmation.
lessons.common_mistakes: Using a Cisco command format on a different switch family.
lessons.practice_task: Run the simulated Aruba version check.
lessons.practice_output:
<SWITCH_NAME>
ArubaOS-CX simulated platform
Version: <SOFTWARE_VERSION>
lessons.final_summary: Choose commands that match the selected switch family.

lessons.title: Read an Aruba Interface
lessons.meaning: Shows a simulated ArubaOS-CX interface summary.
lessons.when_to_use: Use before examining LACP or access VLAN configuration.
lessons.good_output: <ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT> reports up as expected.
lessons.bad_output: A down or blocked member requires focused evidence.
lessons.common_mistakes: Changing LACP membership before reading the bundle.
lessons.next_commands: show lacp interfaces; show running-config interface <ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.practice_task: Read the simulated Aruba interface summary.
lessons.practice_output:
Interface  Status  Speed
<ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT>  up  <SPEED>
lessons.final_summary: Interface state comes before LACP remediation.

lessons.title: Configure an Aruba Access Port
lessons.meaning: Enters simulated ArubaOS-CX configuration mode.
lessons.when_to_use: Only after confirming <ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT>, <VLAN_ID>, approval, and rollback.
lessons.good_output: The selected simulated Aruba port has the expected access VLAN.
lessons.bad_output: Wrong port, wrong VLAN, or no verification.
lessons.common_mistakes: Configuring an uplink as an access port.
lessons.next_commands: interface <ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT>; vlan access <VLAN_ID>; show running-config interface <ARUBA_ACCESS_PORT>
lessons.practice_task: Begin a controlled Aruba access-port simulation.
lessons.practice_output: Enter configuration mode. Changes affect only this local simulation.
lessons.final_summary: Vendor syntax differs, but safety workflow stays the same.

cisco-version.question: What should you learn before troubleshooting a simulated switch?

cisco-interface-status.question: Which state is a warning for an expected endpoint?

cisco-vlan-brief.question: What must you confirm before changing a VLAN assignment?

cisco-mac-table.question: What does a dynamic MAC entry show?
cisco-mac-table.explanation: It maps Layer 2 learning to a port.

hp-irf.question: What does display irf show?
hp-irf.explanation: It is an IRF membership check.

windows-ipconfig.question: What can APIPA suggest?
windows-ipconfig.explanation: It often indicates DHCP or local addressing trouble.

foundation_final.question: What is the safe first step?
Which command checks Cisco port summary?
Which Comware command checks IRF topology?
What should happen before saving a change?
